title: Atoms of Thought: Universal EEG Representation Learning with Microstates
core_claim: A universal EEG tokenizer based on microstates learned from clustered continuous signals improves representation learning across multiple downstream tasks such as sleep staging, emotion recognition, and motor imagery classification.
triage_rationale: The paper has a concrete methodological idea with cross-task evaluation and a plausible interpretability angle, so it is worth a closer look. It is still a relatively specialized EEG representation paper with claims that need full-review scrutiny for dataset breadth, baseline strength, and generalization.
likely_red_flags: Abstract only claims broad improvement; actual baseline coverage and ablations are unknown
Universal tokenizer may depend heavily on the medical EEG pretraining dataset and clustering choices
Interpretability and scalability claims are not substantiated in the abstract
